community.vmware.vmware_guest_file_opera.description: Module to copy a file to a VM, fetch a file from a VM and create or delete a directory in the guest OS.

datacenter.description: The datacenter hosting the virtual machine. If set, it will help to speed up virtual machine search.

cluster.description: The cluster hosting the virtual machine. If set, it will help to speed up virtual machine search.

folder.description: Destination folder, absolute path to find an existing guest or create the new guest. The folder should include the datacenter. ESX's datacenter is ha-datacenter Used only if O(vm_id_type=inventory_path). Examples:    folder: /ha-datacenter/vm    folder: ha-datacenter/vm    folder: /datacenter1/vm    folder: datacenter1/vm    folder: /datacenter1/vm/folder1    folder: datacenter1/vm/folder1    folder: /folder1/datacenter1/vm    folder: folder1/datacenter1/vm    folder: /folder1/datacenter1/vm/folder2    folder: vm/folder2    folder: folder2

vm_id.description: Name of the virtual machine to work with.

vm_id_type.description: The VMware identification method by which the virtual machine will be identified.

vm_username.description: The user to login in to the virtual machine.

vm_password.description: The password used to login-in to the virtual machine.

directory.description: Create or delete a directory. Can be used to create temp directory inside guest using mktemp operation. mktemp sets variable C(dir) in the result with the name of the new directory.

copy.description: Copy file to vm without requiring network.

fetch.description: Get file from virtual machine without requiring network.

timeout.description: Timeout seconds for fetching or copying a file.

community.vmware.vmware_guest_find.short_description: Find the folder path(s) for a virtual machine by name or UUID
community.vmware.vmware_guest_find.description: Find the folder path(s) for a virtual machine by name or UUID

name.description: Name of the VM to work with. This is required if O(uuid) parameter is not supplied.

uuid.description: UUID of the instance to manage if known, this is VMware's BIOS UUID by default. This is required if O(name) parameter is not supplied.

use_instance_uuid.description: Whether to use the VMware instance UUID rather than the BIOS UUID.

community.vmware.vmware_guest_info.short_description: Gather info about a single VM
community.vmware.vmware_guest_info.description: Gather information about a single VM on a VMware ESX cluster.

name.description: Name of the VM to work with This is required if O(uuid) or O(moid) is not supplied.

name_match.description: If multiple VMs matching the name, use the first or last found

uuid.description: UUID of the instance to manage if known, this is VMware's unique identifier. This is required if O(name) or O(moid) is not supplied.

use_instance_uuid.description: Whether to use the VMware instance UUID rather than the BIOS UUID.

moid.description: Managed Object ID of the instance to manage if known, this is a unique identifier only within a single vCenter instance. This is required if O(name) or O(uuid) is not supplied.

ESX's datacenter is ha-datacenter Examples:    folder: /ha-datacenter/vm    folder: ha-datacenter/vm    folder: /datacenter1/vm    folder: datacenter1/vm    folder: /datacenter1/vm/folder1    folder: datacenter1/vm/folder1    folder: /folder1/datacenter1/vm    folder: folder1/datacenter1/vm    folder: /folder1/datacenter1/vm/folder2

tags.description: Whether to show tags or not. If set V(true), shows tags information. Returns a list of tag names. If set V(false), hides tags information. vSphere Automation SDK is required.

tag_details.description: If set V(true), detail information about tags returned. Without this flag, O(tags=true) returns a list of tag names. With this flag, O(tags=true) returns a list of dict about tag information with additional details like category name, category id, and tag id. This parameter is added to maintain backward compatability.

schema.description: Specify the output schema desired. The V(summary) output schema is the legacy output from the module The V(vsphere) output schema is the vSphere API class definition

properties.description: Specify the properties to retrieve. If not specified, all properties are retrieved (deeply). Results are returned in a structure identical to the vsphere API. Example:    properties: [       "config.hardware.memoryMB",       "config.hardware.numCPU",       "guest.disk",       "overallStatus"    ] Only valid when O(schema=vsphere).

community.vmware.vmware_guest_instant_cl.description: This module can be used for Creating a powered-on Instant Clone of a virtual machine. M(community.vmware.vmware_guest) module is needed for creating a VM with poweredon state which would be used as a parent VM. M(vmware.vmware.vm_powerstate) module is also needed to poweroff the instant cloned module. The powered off VM would in turn be deleted by again using M(community.vmware.vmware_guest) module. Thus M(community.vmware.vmware_guest) module is necessary for removing Instant Cloned VM when VMs being created in testing environment. Also GuestOS Customization has now been added with guestinfo_vars parameter. The Parent VM must have The Guest customization Engine for instant Clone to customize Guest OS. Only Linux Os in Parent VM enable support for native vSphere Guest Customization for Instant Clone in vSphere 7.

name.description: Name of the Cloned virtual machine.

parent_vm.description: Name of the parent virtual machine. This is a required parameter, if parameter O(uuid) or O(moid) is not supplied.

uuid.description: UUID of the vm instance to clone from, this is VMware's unique identifier. This is a required parameter, if parameter O(parent_vm) or O(moid) is not supplied.

moid.description: Managed Object ID of the vm instance to manage if known, this is a unique identifier only within a single vCenter instance. This is required if O(parent_vm) or O(uuid) is not supplied.

use_instance_uuid.description: Whether to use the VMware instance UUID rather than the BIOS UUID.

host.description: Name of the ESX Host in datacenter in which to place cloned VM. The host has to be a member of the cluster that contains the resource pool. Required with O(resource_pool) to find resource pool details. This will be used as additional information when there are resource pools with same name.

datastore.description: The name of the datastore or the datastore cluster. If datastore cluster name is specified, module will find the Storage DRS recommended datastore in that cluster.

datacenter.description: Name of the datacenter, where VM to be deployed.

folder.description: Destination folder, absolute path to deploy the cloned vm. Examples: folder: ha-datacenter/vm folder: /datacenter1/vm

resource_pool.description: Name of the resource pool in datacenter in which to place deployed VM. C(Resources) is the default name of resource pool.

vm_username.description: The user to login-in to the virtual machine. Only required when using guest customization feature.

vm_password.description: The password used to login-in to the virtual machine. Only required when using guest customization feature.

guestinfo_vars.description: Provides GuestOS Customization functionality in instant cloned VM. A list of key value pairs that will be passed to the destination VM. These pairs should be used to provide user-defined customization to differentiate the destination VM from the source VM.

wait_vm_tools.description: Whether waiting until vm tools start after rebooting an instant clone vm.

wait_vm_tools_timeout.description: Define a timeout (in seconds) for the O(wait_vm_tools) parameter.

community.vmware.vmware_guest_move.description: This module can be used to move virtual machines between folders.

name.description: Name of the existing virtual machine to move. This is required if O(uuid) or O(moid) is not supplied.

uuid.description: UUID of the virtual machine to manage if known, this is VMware's unique identifier. This is required if O(name) or O(moid) is not supplied.

moid.description: Managed Object ID of the instance to manage if known, this is a unique identifier only within a single vCenter instance. This is required if O(name) or O(uuid) is not supplied.

use_instance_uuid.description: Whether to use the VMware instance UUID rather than the BIOS UUID.

name_match.description: If multiple virtual machines matching the name, use the first or last found.

dest_folder.description: Absolute path to move an existing guest The dest_folder should include the datacenter. ESX's datacenter is ha-datacenter. Examples:    dest_folder: /ha-datacenter/vm    dest_folder: ha-datacenter/vm    dest_folder: /datacenter1/vm    dest_folder: datacenter1/vm    dest_folder: /datacenter1/vm/folder1    dest_folder: datacenter1/vm/folder1    dest_folder: /folder1/datacenter1/vm    dest_folder: folder1/datacenter1/vm    dest_folder: /folder1/datacenter1/vm/folder2

datacenter.description: Destination datacenter for the move operation

community.vmware.vmware_guest_network.short_description: Manage network adapters of specified virtual machine in given vCenter infrastructure
community.vmware.vmware_guest_network.description: This module is used to add, reconfigure, remove network adapter of given virtual machine.

name.description: Name of virtual machine Required if O(uuid) or O(moid) is not supplied.

uuid.description: vm uuid Required if O(name) or O(moid) is not supplied.

use_instance_uuid.description: Whether to use the VMware instance UUID rather than the BIOS UUID.

moid.description: Managed Object ID of the instance to manage if known, this is a unique identifier only within a single vCenter instance. Required if O(uuid) or O(name) is not supplied.

folder.description: Folder location of given VM, this is only required when there's multiple VM's with the same name.

datacenter.description: Datacenter the VM belongs to.

cluster.description: Name of cluster where VM belongs to.

esxi_hostname.description: The hostname of the ESXi host where the VM belongs to.

mac_address.description: MAC address of the NIC that should be altered, if a MAC address is not supplied a new nic will be created. Required when O(state=absent).

label.description: Label of the NIC that should be altered. O(mac_address) or O(label) should be set to get the corresponding device to reconfigure. Alter the name of the network adapter.

vlan_id.description: VLAN id associated with the network.

network_name.description: Name of network in vSphere. Work faster then o(vlan_id) for big infrastructure with many networks.

device_type.description: Type of virtual network device. Valid choices are - V(e1000), V(e1000e), V(pcnet32), V(vmxnet2), V(vmxnet3) (default), V(sriov), V(pvrdma).

pvrdma_device_protocol.description: The PVRDMA device protocol used. Valid choices are - V(rocev1), V(rocev2). This parameter is only used on the VM with hardware version >=14 and <= 19.

switch.description: Name of the (dv)switch for destination network, this is only required for dvswitches.

guest_control.description: Enables guest control over whether the connectable device is connected.

state.description: NIC state. When V(present), a nic will be added if a mac address or label does not previously exists or is unset. When V(absent), the O(mac_address) parameter has to be set.

start_connected.description: If NIC should be connected to network on startup.

connected.description: If NIC should be connected to the network.

directpath_io.description: Enable Universal Pass-through (UPT). Only compatible with the O(device_type=vmxnet3).

physical_function_backing.description: If set, specifies the PCI ID of the physical function to use as backing for a SR-IOV network adapter. This option is only compatible for SR-IOV network adapters.

virtual_function_backing.description: If set, specifies the PCI ID of the physical function to use as backing for a SR-IOV network adapter. This option is only compatible for SR-IOV network adapters.

allow_guest_os_mtu_change.description: Allows the guest OS to change the MTU on a SR-IOV network adapter. This option is only compatible for SR-IOV network adapters.

force.description: Force adapter creation even if an existing adapter is attached to the same network.

gather_network_info.description: Return information about current guest network adapters.

community.vmware.vmware_guest_snapshot.short_description: Manages virtual machines snapshots in vCenter
community.vmware.vmware_guest_snapshot.description: This module can be used to create, delete and update snapshot(s) of the given virtual machine.

state.description: Manage snapshot(s) attached to a specific virtual machine. If set to V(present) and snapshot absent, then will create a new snapshot with the given name. If set to V(present) and snapshot present, then no changes are made. If set to V(absent) and snapshot present, then snapshot with the given name is removed. If set to V(absent) and snapshot absent, then no changes are made. If set to V(revert) and snapshot present, then virtual machine state is reverted to the given snapshot. If set to V(revert) and snapshot absent, then no changes are made. If set to V(remove_all) and snapshot(s) present, then all snapshot(s) will be removed. If set to V(remove_all) and snapshot(s) absent, then no changes are made.

name.description: Name of the virtual machine to work with. This is required parameter, if O(uuid) or O(moid) is not supplied.

name_match.description: If multiple VMs matching the name, use the first or last found.

uuid.description: UUID of the instance to manage if known, this is VMware's BIOS UUID by default. This is required if O(name) or O(moid) parameter is not supplied.

moid.description: Managed Object ID of the instance to manage if known, this is a unique identifier only within a single vCenter instance. This is required if O(name) or O(uuid) is not supplied.

use_instance_uuid.description: Whether to use the VMware instance UUID rather than the BIOS UUID.

folder.description: Destination folder, absolute or relative path to find an existing guest. This is required parameter, if O(name) is supplied. The folder should include the datacenter. ESX's datacenter is ha-datacenter. Examples:    folder: /ha-datacenter/vm    folder: ha-datacenter/vm    folder: /datacenter1/vm    folder: datacenter1/vm    folder: /datacenter1/vm/folder1    folder: datacenter1/vm/folder1    folder: /folder1/datacenter1/vm    folder: folder1/datacenter1/vm    folder: /folder1/datacenter1/vm/folder2

datacenter.description: Destination datacenter for the deploy operation.

snapshot_name.description: Sets the snapshot name to manage. This param or O(snapshot_id) is required only if O(state) is not C(remove_all)

snapshot_id.description: Sets the snapshot id to manage. This param is available when O(state=absent) or O(state=revert).

description.description: Define an arbitrary description to attach to snapshot.

quiesce.description: If set to V(true) and virtual machine is powered on, it will quiesce the file system in virtual machine. Note that VMware Tools are required for this flag. If virtual machine is powered off or VMware Tools are not available, then this flag is set to V(false). If virtual machine does not provide capability to take quiesce snapshot, then this flag is set to V(false).

memory_dump.description: If set to V(true), memory dump of virtual machine is also included in snapshot. Note that memory snapshots take time and resources, this will take longer time to create. If virtual machine does not provide capability to take memory snapshot, then this flag is set to V(false).

remove_children.description: If set to V(true) and O(state=absent), then entire snapshot subtree is set for removal.

new_snapshot_name.description: Value to rename the existing snapshot to.

new_description.description: Value to change the description of an existing snapshot to.

video_memory_mb.description: Valid total MB of video memory range of virtual machine is from 1.172 MB to 256 MB on ESXi 6.7U1, from 1.172 MB to 128 MB on ESXi 6.7 and previous versions. For specific guest OS, supported minimum and maximum video memory are different, please be careful on setting this.

enable_3D.description: Enable 3D for guest operating systems on which VMware supports 3D.

renderer_3D.description: If set to V(automatic), selects the appropriate option (software or hardware) for this virtual machine automatically. If set to V(software), uses normal CPU processing for 3D calculations. If set to V(hardware), requires graphics hardware (GPU) for faster 3D calculations.

memory_3D_mb.description: The value of 3D Memory must be power of 2 and valid value is from 32 MB to 2048 MB.

community.vmware.vmware_host.short_description: Add, remove, or move an ESXi host to, from, or within vCenter
community.vmware.vmware_host.description: This module can be used to add, reconnect, or remove an ESXi host to or from vCenter. This module can also be used to move an ESXi host to a cluster or folder, or vice versa, within the same datacenter.

datacenter_name.description: Name of the datacenter to add the host.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster to add the host. If O(folder) is not set, then this parameter is required.

folder.description: Name of the folder under which host to add. If O(cluster_name) is not set, then this parameter is required. For example, if there is a datacenter 'dc1' under folder called 'Site1' then, this value will be '/Site1/dc1/host'. Here 'host' is an invisible folder under VMware Web Client. Another example, if there is a nested folder structure like '/myhosts/india/pune' under datacenter 'dc2', then O(folder) value will be '/dc2/host/myhosts/india/pune'. Other Examples: '/Site2/dc2/Asia-Cluster/host' or '/dc3/Asia-Cluster/host'

add_connected.description: If set to V(true), then the host should be connected as soon as it is added. This parameter is ignored if not O(state=present).

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname to manage.

esxi_username.description: ESXi username. Required for adding a host. Optional for reconnect. If both O(esxi_username) and O(esxi_password) are used Unused for removing. No longer a required parameter from version 2.5.

esxi_password.description: ESXi password. Required for adding a host. Optional for reconnect. Unused for removing. No longer a required parameter from version 2.5.

state.description: If set to V(present), add the host if host is absent. If set to V(present), update the location of the host if host already exists. If set to V(absent), remove the host if host is present. If set to V(absent), do nothing if host already does not exists. If set to V(add_or_reconnect), add the host if it's absent else reconnect it and update the location. If set to V(reconnect), then reconnect the host if it's present and update the location. If set to V(disconnected), disconnect the host if the host already exists.

esxi_ssl_thumbprint.description: Specifying the hostsystem certificate's thumbprint. Use following command to get hostsystem certificate's thumbprint -  # openssl x509 -in /etc/vmware/ssl/rui.crt -fingerprint -sha1 -noout

fetch_ssl_thumbprint.description: Fetch the thumbprint of the host's SSL certificate. This basically disables the host certificate verification (check if it was signed by a recognized CA). Disable this option if you want to allow only hosts with valid certificates to be added to vCenter. If this option is set to V(false) and the certificate can't be verified, an add or reconnect will fail. Unused when O(esxi_ssl_thumbprint) is set. Optional for reconnect, but only used if O(esxi_username) and O(esxi_password) are used. Unused for removing.

force_connection.description: Force the connection if the host is already being managed by another vCenter server.

reconnect_disconnected.description: Reconnect disconnected hosts. This is only used if O(state=present) and if the host already exists.

community.vmware.vmware_host.deprecated: This module has been moved to the L(new vmware.vmware collection,https://forum.ansible.com/t/5880)

community.vmware.vmware_host_acceptance.short_description: Manage the host acceptance level of an ESXi host
community.vmware.vmware_host_acceptance.description: This module can be used to manage the host acceptance level of an ESXi host. The host acceptance level controls the acceptance level of each VIB on a ESXi host.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster. Acceptance level of all ESXi host system in the given cluster will be managed. If O(esxi_hostname) is not given, this parameter is required.

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname. Acceptance level of this ESXi host system will be managed. If O(cluster_name) is not given, this parameter is required.

state.description: If set to V(partner), then accept only partner and VMware signed and certified VIBs. If set to V(vmware_certified), then accept only VIBs that are signed and certified by VMware. If set to V(vmware_accepted), then accept VIBs that have been accepted by VMware. If set to V(community), then accept all VIBs, even those that are not signed.

community.vmware.vmware_host_acceptance_.short_description: Gather host acceptance level of ESXi hosts
community.vmware.vmware_host_acceptance_.description: This module can be used to gather the host acceptance level of ESXi hosts. The host acceptance level controls the acceptance level of each VIB on a ESXi host.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster. Acceptance level of all ESXi host system in the given cluster will be gathered. If O(esxi_hostname) is not given, this parameter is required.

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname. Acceptance level of this ESXi host system will be gathered. If O(cluster_name) is not given, this parameter is required.

community.vmware.vmware_host_active_dire.short_description: Joins an ESXi host system to an Active Directory domain or leaves it
community.vmware.vmware_host_active_dire.description: This module can be used to join or leave an ESXi host to or from an Active Directory domain.

ad_state.description: Whether the ESXi host is joined to an AD domain or not.

esxi_hostname.description: Name of the host system to work with. This parameter is required if O(cluster_name) is not specified.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster from which all host systems will be used. This parameter is required if O(esxi_hostname) is not specified.

community.vmware.vmware_host_auto_start.short_description: Manage the auto power ON or OFF for vm on ESXi host
community.vmware.vmware_host_auto_start.description: In this module, can set up automatic startup and shutdown of virtual machines according to host startup or shutdown.

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname where the VM to set auto power on or off exists.

name.description: VM name to set auto power on or off. This is not necessary if change only system default VM settings for autoStart config.

uuid.description: VM uuid to set auto power on or off, this is VMware's unique identifier. This is required if O(name) is not supplied. This is not necessary if change only system default VM settings for autoStart config.

use_instance_uuid.description: Whether to use the VMware instance UUID rather than the BIOS UUID.

moid.description: Managed Object ID of the instance to manage if known, this is a unique identifier only within a single vCenter instance. This is required if O(name) or O(uuid) is not supplied.

system_defaults.description: System defaults for auto-start or auto-stop config for virtual machine.

power_info.description: Startup or shutdown settings of virtual machine. This setting will override the system defaults.

community.vmware.vmware_host_capability_.short_description: Gathers info about an ESXi host's capability information
community.vmware.vmware_host_capability_.description: This module can be used to gather information about an ESXi host's capability information when ESXi hostname or Cluster name is given.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster from all host systems to be used for information gathering. If O(esxi_hostname) is not given, this parameter is required.

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname to gather information from. If O(cluster_name) is not given, this parameter is required.

community.vmware.vmware_host_config_info.short_description: Gathers info about an ESXi host's advance configuration information
community.vmware.vmware_host_config_info.description: This module can be used to gather information about an ESXi host's advance configuration information when ESXi hostname or Cluster name is given.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster from which the ESXi host belong to. If O(esxi_hostname) is not given, this parameter is required.

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname to gather information from. If O(cluster_name) is not given, this parameter is required.

community.vmware.vmware_host_config_mana.short_description: Manage advanced system settings of an ESXi host
community.vmware.vmware_host_config_mana.description: This module can be used to manage advanced system settings of an ESXi host when ESXi hostname or Cluster name is given.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster. Settings are applied to every ESXi host in given cluster. If O(esxi_hostname) is not given, this parameter is required.

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname. Settings are applied to this ESXi host. If O(cluster_name) is not given, this parameter is required.

options.description: A dictionary of advanced system settings. Invalid options will cause module to error. Note that the list of advanced options (with description and values) can be found by running `vim-cmd hostsvc/advopt/options`.

community.vmware.vmware_host_custom_attr.short_description: Manage custom attributes from VMware for the given ESXi host
community.vmware.vmware_host_custom_attr.description: This module can be used to add, remove and update custom attributes for the given ESXi host.

esxi_hostname.description: Name of the ESXi host to work with. This is a required parameter

state.description: The action to take. If set to V(present), then custom attribute is added or updated. If set to V(absent), then custom attribute is removed.

attributes.description: A list of name and value of custom attributes that needs to be manage. Value of custom attribute is not required and will be ignored, if O(state=absent).

community.vmware.vmware_host_datastore.short_description: Manage a datastore on ESXi host
community.vmware.vmware_host_datastore.description: This module can be used to mount/umount datastore on ESXi host. This module only supports NFS (NFS v3 or NFS v4.1) and VMFS datastores. For VMFS datastore, available device must already be connected on ESXi host.

datastore_name.description: Name of the datastore to add/remove.

datastore_type.description: Type of the datastore to configure (nfs/nfs41/vmfs/vvol).

nfs_server.description: NFS host serving nfs datastore. Required if O(datastore_type=nfs) / O(datastore_type=nfs41) and O(state=present), else unused. Two or more servers can be defined if O(datastore_type=nfs41)

nfs_path.description: Resource path on NFS host. Required if O(datastore_type=nfs) / O(datastore_type=nfs41) and O(state=present), else unused.

nfs_ro.description: ReadOnly or ReadWrite mount. Unused if datastore type is not set to O(datastore_type=nfs) / O(datastore_type=nfs41) and not O(state=present).

resignature.description: Allows forcing resignature of unresolved VMFS datastore that already exists on the specified disk device. Unused if not O(datastore_type=vmfs) and not O(state=present).

vmfs_device_name.description: Name of the device to be used as VMFS datastore. Required for VMFS datastore type and O(state=present), else unused.

vmfs_version.description: VMFS version to use for datastore creation. Unused if not O(datastore_type=vmfs) and not O(state=present).

vasa_provider.description: hostname or ipaddress of the VASA provider to use for vVols provisioning

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname to manage the datastore. Required when used with a vcenter

auto_expand.description: Expand a datastore capacity to full if it has free capacity. This parameter can't be extend using another datastore. Can be used to expand a datastore capacity after increasing LUN volume.

state.description: present: Mount datastore on host if datastore is absent else do nothing. absent: Umount datastore if datastore is present else do nothing.

community.vmware.vmware_host_disk_info.short_description: Gathers information about disks attached to given ESXi host/s.
community.vmware.vmware_host_disk_info.description: This module returns information about disks attached to given ESXi host/s If O(cluster_name) is provided, then disk information about all hosts from the given cluster will be returned. If O(esxi_hostname) is provided, then disk information about the given host system will be returned.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster from which the ESXi host belong to. If O(esxi_hostname) is not given, this parameter is required.

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname to gather information from. If O(cluster_name) is not given, this parameter is required.

community.vmware.vmware_host_dns.short_description: Manage DNS configuration of an ESXi host system
community.vmware.vmware_host_dns.description: This module can be used to configure DNS for the default TCP/IP stack on an ESXi host system.

type.description: Type of DNS assignment. Either V(dhcp) or V(static). A VMkernel adapter needs to be set to DHCP if set to V(dhcp).

device.description: The VMkernel network adapter to obtain DNS settings from. Needs to get its IP through DHCP, a static network configuration combined with a dynamic DNS configuration doesn't work. The parameter is only required in case of O(type=dhcp).

host_name.description: The hostname to be used for the ESXi host. Cannot be used when configuring a complete cluster.

domain.description: The domain name to be used for the ESXi host.

dns_servers.description: A list of DNS servers to be used. The order of the DNS servers is important as they are used consecutively in order.

search_domains.description: A list of domains to be searched through by the resolver.

verbose.description: Verbose output of the DNS server configuration change. Explains if an DNS server was added, removed, or if the DNS server sequence was changed.

esxi_hostname.description: Name of the host system to work with. This parameter is required if O(cluster_name) is not specified and you connect to a vCenter. Cannot be used when you connect directly to an ESXi host.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster from which all host systems will be used. This parameter is required if O(esxi_hostname) is not specified and you connect to a vCenter. Cannot be used when you connect directly to an ESXi host.

community.vmware.vmware_host_dns_info.short_description: Gathers info about an ESXi host's DNS configuration information
community.vmware.vmware_host_dns_info.description: This module can be used to gather information about an ESXi host's DNS configuration information when ESXi hostname or Cluster name is given.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster from which the ESXi host belong to. If O(esxi_hostname) is not given, this parameter is required.

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname to gather information from. If O(cluster_name) is not given, this parameter is required.

community.vmware.vmware_host_facts.short_description: Gathers facts about remote ESXi hostsystem
community.vmware.vmware_host_facts.description: This module can be used to gathers facts like CPU, memory, datastore, network and system etc. about ESXi host system. Please specify hostname or IP address of ESXi host system as O(hostname). If hostname or IP address of vCenter is provided as O(hostname) and O(esxi_hostname) is not specified, then the module will throw an error. Please note that when ESXi host connection state is not C(connected), facts returned from vCenter might be stale. Users are recommended to check connection state value and take appropriate decision in the playbook.

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname. Host facts about the specified ESXi server will be returned. By specifying this option, you can select which ESXi hostsystem is returned if connecting to a vCenter.

show_tag.description: Tags related to Host are shown if set to V(true).

show_datacenter.description: Return the related Datacenter if set to V(true).

schema.description: Specify the output schema desired. The V(summary) output schema is the legacy output from the module The V(vsphere) output schema is the vSphere API class definition

properties.description: Specify the properties to retrieve. If not specified, all properties are retrieved (deeply). Results are returned in a structure identical to the vsphere API. Example:    properties: [       "hardware.memorySize",       "hardware.cpuInfo.numCpuCores",       "config.product.apiVersion",       "overallStatus"    ] Only valid when O(schema=vsphere).

community.vmware.vmware_host_feature_inf.short_description: Gathers info about an ESXi host's feature capability information
community.vmware.vmware_host_feature_inf.description: This module can be used to gather information about an ESXi host's feature capability information when ESXi hostname or Cluster name is given.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster from all host systems to be used for information gathering. If O(esxi_hostname) is not given, this parameter is required.

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname to gather information from. If O(cluster_name) is not given, this parameter is required.

community.vmware.vmware_host_firewall_in.short_description: Gathers info about an ESXi host's firewall configuration information
community.vmware.vmware_host_firewall_in.description: This module can be used to gather information about an ESXi host's firewall configuration information when ESXi hostname or Cluster name is given.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster from which the ESXi host belong to. If O(esxi_hostname) is not given, this parameter is required.

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname to gather information from. If O(cluster_name) is not given, this parameter is required.

community.vmware.vmware_host_firewall_ma.short_description: Manage firewall configurations about an ESXi host
community.vmware.vmware_host_firewall_ma.description: This module can be used to manage firewall configurations about an ESXi host when ESXi hostname or Cluster name is given.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster. Firewall settings are applied to every ESXi host system in given cluster. If O(esxi_hostname) is not given, this parameter is required.

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname. Firewall settings are applied to this ESXi host system. If O(cluster_name) is not given, this parameter is required.

rules.description: A list of Rule set which needs to be managed. Each member of list is rule set name and state to be set the rule. Both rule name and rule state are required parameters. Additional IPs and networks can also be specified Please see examples for more information.

community.vmware.vmware_host_graphics.description: This module can be used to manage Host Graphic Settings

cluster_name.description: Name of cluster. All host system from given cluster used to manage Host Graphic Settings. Required parameter, if O(esxi_hostname) is not set.

esxi_hostname.description: List of ESXi hostname to manage Host Graphic Settings. Required parameter, if O(cluster_name) is not set.

restart_xorg.description: Restart X.Org Server after change any parameter ( O(graphic_type) or O(assigment_policy) )

community.vmware.vmware_host_hyperthread.short_description: Enables/Disables Hyperthreading optimization for an ESXi host system
community.vmware.vmware_host_hyperthread.description: This module can be used to enable or disable Hyperthreading optimization for ESXi host systems in given vCenter infrastructure. It also checks if Hyperthreading is activated/deactivated and if the host needs to be restarted. The module informs the user if Hyperthreading is enabled but inactive because the processor is vulnerable to L1 Terminal Fault (L1TF).

state.description: Enable or disable Hyperthreading. You need to reboot the ESXi host if you change the configuration. Make sure that Hyperthreading is enabled in the BIOS. Otherwise, it will be enabled, but never activated.

esxi_hostname.description: Name of the host system to work with. This parameter is required if O(cluster_name) is not specified.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster from which all host systems will be used. This parameter is required if O(esxi_hostname) is not specified.

community.vmware.vmware_host_ipv6.short_description: Enables/Disables IPv6 support for an ESXi host system
community.vmware.vmware_host_ipv6.description: This module can be used to enable or disable IPv6 support for ESXi host systems in given vCenter infrastructure. It also checks if the host needs to be restarted.

state.description: Enable or disable IPv6 support. You need to reboot the ESXi host if you change the configuration.

esxi_hostname.description: Name of the host system to work with. This is required parameter if O(cluster_name) is not specified.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster from which all host systems will be used. This is required parameter if O(esxi_hostname) is not specified.

community.vmware.vmware_host_iscsi.short_description: Manage the iSCSI configuration of ESXi host
community.vmware.vmware_host_iscsi.description: In this module, can manage the iSCSI configuration of ESXi host

esxi_hostname.description: The ESXi hostname on which to change iSCSI settings.

iscsi_config.description: The iSCSI configs. This parameter is required if O(state=present) or O(state=absent).

state.description: If set to V(present), add the iSCSI target or the bind ports if they are not existing. If set to V(present), update the iSCSI settings if they already exist and occur change. If set to V(absent), remove the iSCSI target or the bind ports if they are existing. If set to V(enabled), enable the iSCSI of ESXi if the iSCSI is disabled. If set to V(disabled), disable the iSCSI of ESXi if the iSCSI is enabled.

community.vmware.vmware_host_iscsi_info.short_description: Gather iSCSI configuration information of ESXi host
community.vmware.vmware_host_iscsi_info.description: This module can be used to gather information about the iSCSI configuration of the ESXi host.

esxi_hostname.description: The ESXi hostname on which to gather iSCSI settings.

community.vmware.vmware_host_kernel_mana.short_description: Manage kernel module options on ESXi hosts
community.vmware.vmware_host_kernel_mana.description: This module can be used to manage kernel module options on ESXi hosts. All connected ESXi hosts in scope will be configured when specified. If a host is not connected at time of configuration, it will be marked as such in the output. Kernel module options may require a reboot to take effect which is not covered here. You can use M(ansible.builtin.reboot) or M(community.vmware.vmware_host_powerstate) module to reboot all ESXi host systems.

esxi_hostname.description: Name of the ESXi host to work on. This parameter is required if O(cluster_name) is not specified.

cluster_name.description: Name of the VMware cluster to work on. All ESXi hosts in this cluster will be configured. This parameter is required if O(esxi_hostname) is not specified.

kernel_module_name.description: Name of the kernel module to be configured.

kernel_module_option.description: Specified configurations will be applied to the given module. These values are specified in key=value pairs and separated by a space when there are multiple options.

community.vmware.vmware_host_lockdown.short_description: Manage administrator permission for the local administrative account for the ESXi host
community.vmware.vmware_host_lockdown.description: This module can be used to manage administrator permission for the local administrative account for the host when ESXi hostname is given. This module is destructive as administrator permission are managed using APIs used, please read options carefully and proceed. Please specify O(hostname) as vCenter IP or hostname only, as lockdown operations are not possible from standalone ESXi server.

cluster_name.description: Name of cluster. All host systems from given cluster used to manage lockdown. Required parameter, if O(esxi_hostname) is not set.

esxi_hostname.description: List of ESXi hostname to manage lockdown. Required parameter, if O(cluster_name) is not set. See examples for specifications.

state.description: State of hosts system If set to V(disabled), all host systems will be removed from lockdown mode. If host system is already out of lockdown mode and set to V(disabled), no action will be taken. If set to V(normal), all host systems will be set in lockdown mode. If host system is already in lockdown mode and set to V(normal), no action will be taken. If set to V(strict), all host systems will be set in strict lockdown mode. If host system is already in strict lockdown mode and set to V(strict), no action will be taken.

community.vmware.vmware_host_lockdown_ex.description: This module can be used to manage Lockdown Mode Exception Users. Please specify O(hostname) as vCenter IP or hostname only, as lockdown operations are not possible from standalone ESXi server.

cluster_name.description: Name of cluster. All host systems from given cluster used to manage exception users. Required parameter, if O(esxi_hostname) is not set.

esxi_hostname.description: List of ESXi hostname to manage exception users. Required parameter, if O(cluster_name) is not set.

state.description: If V(present), make sure the given users are defined as Lockdown Mode Exception Users. If V(absent), make sure the given users are NO Lockdown Mode Exception Users. If V(set), will replace Lockdown Mode Exception Users defined list of users.

exception_users.description: List of Lockdown Mode Exception Users. To remove all Exception Users, O(state=set) the empty list.

community.vmware.vmware_host_logbundle.description: This module can be used to fetch logbundle file from ESXi.

esxi_hostname.description: Name of the host system to fetch the logbundle.

dest.description: file destination on localhost, path must be exist.

download_timeout.description: The user defined timeout in seconds of exporting the log file. The default of the function this module uses is so low that you have to set this this to a higher value in all probabilty.

manifests.description: Logs to include in the logbundle file. Refer to the id key of the M(community.vmware.vmware_host_logbundle_info) module for values that can be specified in the manifest.

community.vmware.vmware_host_logbundle_i.description: This module can be used to gather manifest information for logbundle from ESXi.

esxi_hostname.description: Name of the host system to fetch the manifests for logbundle.

community.vmware.vmware_host_ntp.short_description: Manage NTP server configuration of an ESXi host
community.vmware.vmware_host_ntp.description: This module can be used to configure, add or remove NTP servers from an ESXi host. If O(state) is not given, the NTP servers will be configured in the exact sequence. User can specify an ESXi hostname or Cluster name. In case of cluster name, all ESXi hosts are updated.

esxi_hostname.description: Name of the host system to work with. This parameter is required if O(cluster_name) is not specified.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster from which all host systems will be used. This parameter is required if O(esxi_hostname) is not specified.

ntp_servers.description: IP or FQDN of NTP server(s). This accepts a list of NTP servers. For multiple servers, please look at the examples.

state.description: V(present): Add NTP server(s), if specified server(s) are absent else do nothing. V(absent): Remove NTP server(s), if specified server(s) are present else do nothing. Specified NTP server(s) will be configured if O(state) isn't specified.

verbose.description: Verbose output of the configuration change. Explains if an NTP server was added, removed, or if the NTP server sequence was changed.

community.vmware.vmware_host_ntp_info.short_description: Gathers info about NTP configuration on an ESXi host
community.vmware.vmware_host_ntp_info.description: This module can be used to gather information about NTP configurations on an ESXi host.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster. NTP config information about each ESXi server will be returned for the given cluster. If O(esxi_hostname) is not given, this parameter is required.

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname. NTP config information about this ESXi server will be returned. If O(cluster_name) is not given, this parameter is required.

community.vmware.vmware_host_package_inf.short_description: Gathers info about available packages on an ESXi host
community.vmware.vmware_host_package_inf.description: This module can be used to gather information about available packages and their status on an ESXi host.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster. Package information about each ESXi server will be returned for given cluster. If O(esxi_hostname) is not given, this parameter is required.

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname. Package information about this ESXi server will be returned. If O(cluster_name) is not given, this parameter is required.

community.vmware.vmware_host_passthrough.short_description: Manage PCI device passthrough settings on host
community.vmware.vmware_host_passthrough.description: This module can be managed PCI device passthrough settings on host.

cluster.description: Name of the cluster from which all host systems will be used. This parameter is required if O(esxi_hostname) is not specified.

esxi_hostname.description: Name of the host system to work with. This parameter is required if O(cluster_name) is not specified. User can specify specific host from the cluster.

devices.description: List of PCI device name or id.

state.description: If V(state=present), passthrough of PCI device will be enabled. If V(state=absent), passthrough of PCI device will be disabled.

community.vmware.vmware_host_powermgmt_p.short_description: Manages the Power Management Policy of an ESXI host system
community.vmware.vmware_host_powermgmt_p.description: This module can be used to manage the Power Management Policy of ESXi host systems in given vCenter infrastructure.

policy.description: Set the Power Management Policy of the host system.

esxi_hostname.description: Name of the host system to work with. This is required parameter if O(cluster_name) is not specified.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster from which all host systems will be used. This is required parameter if O(esxi_hostname) is not specified.

community.vmware.vmware_host_powerstate.short_description: Manages power states of host systems in vCenter
community.vmware.vmware_host_powerstate.description: This module can be used to manage power states of host systems in given vCenter infrastructure. User can set power state to 'power-down-to-standby', 'power-up-from-standby', 'shutdown-host' and 'reboot-host'. State 'reboot-host', 'shutdown-host' and 'power-down-to-standby' are not supported by all the host systems.

state.description: Set the state of the host system.

esxi_hostname.description: Name of the host system to work with. This is required parameter if O(cluster_name) is not specified.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster from which all host systems will be used. This is required parameter if O(esxi_hostname) is not specified.

force.description: This parameter specify if the host should be proceeding with user defined powerstate regardless of whether it is in maintenance mode. If O(state=reboot-host) and O(force=true), then host system is rebooted regardless of whether it is in maintenance mode. If O(state=shutdown-host) and O(force=true), then host system is shutdown regardless of whether it is in maintenance mode. If O(state=power-down-to-standby) and O(force=true), then all powered off VMs will evacuated. Not applicable if O(state=power-up-from-standby).

timeout.description: This parameter defines timeout for O(state=power-down-to-standby) or O(state=power-up-from-standby). Ignored if O(state=reboot-host) or O(state=shutdown-host). This parameter is defined in seconds.

community.vmware.vmware_host_scanhba.short_description: Rescan host HBA's and optionally refresh the storage system
community.vmware.vmware_host_scanhba.description: This module can force a rescan of the hosts HBA subsystem which is needed when wanting to mount a new datastore. You could use this before using M(community.vmware.vmware_host_datastore) to mount a new datastore to ensure your device/volume is ready. You can also optionally force a Refresh of the Storage System in vCenter/ESXi Web Client. You can supply an esxi_hostname or a cluster_name

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname to Rescan the storage subsystem on.

cluster_name.description: Cluster name to Rescan the storage subsystem on (this will run the rescan task on each host in the cluster).

rescan_hba.description: Rescan all host bus adapters for new storage devices. Rescanning all adapters can be slow.

refresh_storage.description: Refresh the storage system in vCenter/ESXi Web Client for each host found

rescan_vmfs.description: Rescan all known storage devices for new VMFS volumes.

community.vmware.vmware_host_scsidisk_in.short_description: Gather information about SCSI disk attached to the given ESXi
community.vmware.vmware_host_scsidisk_in.description: This module can be used to gather information about SCSI disk attached to the given ESXi.

esxi_hostname.description: Name of the host system to work with. SCSI disk information about this ESXi server will be returned. This parameter is required if O(cluster_name) is not specified.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster from which all host systems will be used. SCSI disk information about each ESXi server will be returned for the given cluster. This parameter is required if O(esxi_hostname) is not specified.

community.vmware.vmware_host_service_inf.short_description: Gathers info about an ESXi host's services
community.vmware.vmware_host_service_inf.description: This module can be used to gather information about an ESXi host's services.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster. Service information about each ESXi server will be returned for given cluster. If O(esxi_hostname) is not given, this parameter is required.

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname. Service information about this ESXi server will be returned. If O(cluster_name) is not given, this parameter is required.

community.vmware.vmware_host_service_man.short_description: Manage services on a given ESXi host
community.vmware.vmware_host_service_man.description: This module can be used to manage (start, stop, restart) services on a given ESXi host. If cluster_name is provided, specified service will be managed on all ESXi host belonging to that cluster. If specific esxi_hostname is provided, then specified service will be managed on given ESXi host only.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster. Service settings are applied to every ESXi host system/s in given cluster. If O(esxi_hostname) is not given, this parameter is required.

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname. Service settings are applied to this ESXi host system. If O(cluster_name) is not given, this parameter is required.

state.description: Desired state of service. V(start) and V(present) has same effect. V(stop) and V(absent) has same effect. V(unchanged) allows defining O(service_policy) without defining or changing service state.

service_policy.description: Set of valid service policy strings. If set V(on), then service should be started when the host starts up. If set V(automatic), then service should run if and only if it has open firewall ports. If set V(off), then Service should not be started when the host starts up.

service_name.description: Name of Service to be managed. This is a brief identifier for the service, for example, ntpd, vxsyslogd etc. This value should be a valid ESXi service name.

community.vmware.vmware_host_snmp.short_description: Configures SNMP on an ESXi host system
community.vmware.vmware_host_snmp.description: This module can be used to configure the embedded SNMP agent on an ESXi host.

cluster_name.description: Name of cluster. All host system from given cluster used to manage SNMP agent. Required parameter, if O(esxi_hostname) is not set.

esxi_hostname.description: List of ESXi hostname to manage SNMP agent. Required parameter, if O(cluster_name) is not set.

state.description: Enable, disable, or reset the SNMP agent.

snmp_port.description: Port used by the SNMP agent.

trap_targets.description: A list of trap targets. You need to use C(hostname), C(port), and C(community) for each trap target.

trap_filter.description: A list of trap oids for traps not to be sent by agent, e.g. [ 1.3.6.1.4.1.6876.4.1.1.0, 1.3.6.1.4.1.6876.4.1.1.1 ] Use value V(reset) to clear settings.

send_trap.description: Send a test trap to validate the configuration.

hw_source.description: Source hardware events from IPMI sensors or CIM Indications. The embedded SNMP agent receives hardware events either from IPMI sensors V(sensors) or CIM indications V(indications).

sys_contact.description: System contact who manages the system.

community.vmware.vmware_host_sriov.description: This module can be used to configure, enable or disable SR-IOV functions on ESXi host. Module does not reboot the host after changes, but puts it in output "rebootRequired" state. User can specify an ESXi hostname or Cluster name. In case of cluster name, all ESXi hosts are updated.

esxi_hostname.description: Name of the host system to work with. This parameter is required if O(cluster_name) is not specified. User can specify specific host from the cluster.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster from which all host systems will be used. This parameter is required if O(esxi_hostname) is not specified.

num_virt_func.description: number of functions to activate on interface. 0 means SR-IOV disabled. number greater than 0 means SR-IOV enabled.

sriov_on.description: optional parameter, related to O(num_virt_func). SR-IOV can be enabled only if O(num_virt_func) > 0.

community.vmware.vmware_host_ssl_info.short_description: Gather info of ESXi host system about SSL
community.vmware.vmware_host_ssl_info.description: This module can be used to gather information of the SSL thumbprint information for a host.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster. SSL thumbprint information about all ESXi host system in the given cluster will be reported. If O(esxi_hostname) is not given, this parameter is required.

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname. SSL thumbprint information of this ESXi host system will be reported. If O(cluster_name) is not given, this parameter is required.

community.vmware.vmware_host_tcpip_stack.short_description: Manage the TCP/IP Stacks configuration of ESXi host
community.vmware.vmware_host_tcpip_stack.description: This module can be used to modify the TCP/IP stacks configuration.

esxi_hostname.description: Name of the ESXi host.

default.description: The TCP/IP stacks configuration of the I(default).

provisioning.description: The TCP/IP stacks configuration of the I(provisioning).

vmotion.description: The TCP/IP stacks configuration of the I(vmotion).

vxlan.description: The TCP/IP stacks configuration of the I(vxlan).

community.vmware.vmware_host_user_manage.description: This module can add, update or delete local users on ESXi host.

esxi_hostname.description: Name of the ESXi host that is managing the local user.

user_password.description: The local user password. If you'd like to update the password, requires O(override_user_password=true).

override_user_password.description: If the local user exists and updates the password, change this parameter value is true.

state.description: If set to V(present), add a new local user or update information. If set to V(absent), delete the local user.

community.vmware.vmware_host_vmhba_info.short_description: Gathers info about vmhbas available on the given ESXi host
community.vmware.vmware_host_vmhba_info.description: This module can be used to gather information about vmhbas available on the given ESXi host. If O(cluster_name) is provided, then vmhba information about all hosts from given cluster will be returned. If O(esxi_hostname) is provided, then vmhba information about given host system will be returned.

esxi_hostname.description: Name of the host system to work with. Vmhba information about this ESXi server will be returned. This parameter is required if O(cluster_name) is not specified.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster from which all host systems will be used. Vmhba information about each ESXi server will be returned for the given cluster. This parameter is required if O(esxi_hostname) is not specified.

community.vmware.vmware_host_vmnic_info.short_description: Gathers info about vmnics available on the given ESXi host
community.vmware.vmware_host_vmnic_info.description: This module can be used to gather information about vmnics available on the given ESXi host. If O(cluster_name) is provided, then vmnic information about all hosts from given cluster will be returned. If O(esxi_hostname) is provided, then vmnic information about given host system will be returned.

capabilities.description: Gather information about general capabilities (Auto negotiation, Wake On LAN, and Network I/O Control).

directpath_io.description: Gather information about DirectPath I/O capabilities and configuration.

sriov.description: Gather information about SR-IOV capabilities and configuration.

esxi_hostname.description: Name of the host system to work with. Vmnic information about this ESXi server will be returned. This parameter is required if O(cluster_name) is not specified.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster from which all host systems will be used. Vmnic information about each ESXi server will be returned for the given cluster. This parameter is required if O(esxi_hostname) is not specified.

community.vmware.vmware_local_role_info.short_description: Gather info about local roles on an ESXi host or vCenter
community.vmware.vmware_local_role_info.description: This module can be used to gather information about local role info on an ESXi host or vCenter

community.vmware.vmware_local_role_manag.short_description: Manage local roles on an ESXi host or vCenter
community.vmware.vmware_local_role_manag.description: This module can be used to manage local roles on an ESXi host or vCenter.

local_role_name.description: The local role name to be managed.

local_privilege_ids.description: The list of privileges that role needs to have. Please see U(https://docs.vmware.com/en/VMware-vSphere/6.0/com.vmware.vsphere.security.doc/GUID-ED56F3C4-77D0-49E3-88B6-B99B8B437B62.html)

state.description: Indicate desired state of the role. If the role already exists when V(present), the role info is updated.

force_remove.description: If set to V(false) then prevents the role from being removed if any permissions are using it.

action.description: This parameter is only valid while updating an existing role with privileges. V(add) will add the privileges to the existing privilege list. V(remove) will remove the privileges from the existing privilege list. V(set) will replace the privileges of the existing privileges with user defined list of privileges.

community.vmware.vmware_local_user_info.short_description: Gather info about users on the given ESXi host
community.vmware.vmware_local_user_info.description: This module can be used to gather information about users present on the given ESXi host system in VMware infrastructure. User must hold the 'Authorization.ModifyPermissions' privilege to invoke this module.

community.vmware.vmware_local_user_manag.short_description: Manage local users on an ESXi host
community.vmware.vmware_local_user_manag.description: Manage local users on an ESXi host

local_user_name.description: The local user name to be changed.

state.description: Indicate desired state of the user. If the user already exists when V(present), the user info is updated

community.vmware.vmware_maintenancemode.short_description: Place a host into maintenance mode
community.vmware.vmware_maintenancemode.description: This module can be used for placing a ESXi host into maintenance mode. Support for VSAN compliant maintenance mode when selected.

esxi_hostname.description: Name of the host as defined in vCenter.

vsan.description: Specify which VSAN compliant mode to enter.

evacuate.description: If set to V(true), evacuate all powered off VMs.

timeout.description: Specify a timeout for the operation.

state.description: Enter or exit maintenance mode.

community.vmware.vmware_maintenancemode.deprecated: This module has been moved to the L(new vmware.vmware collection,https://forum.ansible.com/t/5880)

community.vmware.vmware_migrate_vmk.short_description: Migrate a VMK interface from VSS to VDS
community.vmware.vmware_migrate_vmk.description: Migrate a VMK interface from VSS to VDS

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname to be managed

current_switch_name.description: Switch VMK interface is currently on

current_portgroup_name.description: Portgroup name VMK interface is currently on

migrate_switch_name.description: Switch name to migrate VMK interface to

migrate_portgroup_name.description: Portgroup name to migrate VMK interface to

migrate_vlan_id.description: VLAN to use for the VMK interface when migrating from VDS to VSS Will be ignored when migrating from VSS to VDS

community.vmware.vmware_object_custom_at.short_description: Gather custom attributes of an object
community.vmware.vmware_object_custom_at.description: This module can be gathered custom attributes of an object.

object_type.description: Type of an object to work with.

object_name.description: Name of the object to work with.

moid.description: Managed Object ID of the instance to get if known, this is a unique identifier only within a single vCenter instance. This is required if O(object_name) is not supplied.

community.vmware.vmware_object_rename.description: This module can be used to rename VMware objects. Renaming Host and Network is not supported by VMware APIs.

object_type.description: Type of object to work with. Valid options are Cluster, ClusterComputeResource, Datacenter, Datastore, Folder, ResourcePool, VM or VirtualMachine.

object_name.description: Name of the object to work with. Mutually exclusive with O(object_moid).

object_moid.description: Managed object id of the VMware object to work with. Mutually exclusive with O(object_name).

community.vmware.vmware_object_role_perm.short_description: Manage local roles on an ESXi host or vCenter
community.vmware.vmware_object_role_perm.description: This module can be used to manage object permissions on the given host or vCenter.

role.description: The role to be assigned permission. User can also specify role name presented in Web UI.

principal.description: The user to be assigned permission. Required if O(group) is not specified. If specifying domain user, required separator of domain uses backslash.

group.description: The group to be assigned permission. Required if O(principal) is not specified.

object_name.description: The object name to assigned permission.

recursive.description: Should the permissions be recursively applied.

state.description: Indicate desired state of the object's permission. When V(present), the permission will be added if it doesn't already exist. When V(absent), the permission is removed if it exists.

community.vmware.vmware_object_role_perm.description: This module can be used to gather object permissions on the given VMware object.

principal.description: The optional name of an entity, such as a user, assigned permissions on an object. If provided, actual permissions on the specified object are returned for the principal, instead of roles.

object_name.description: The object name to assigned permission. Mutually exclusive with O(moid).

moid.description: Managed object ID for the given object. Mutually exclusive with O(object_name).

community.vmware.vmware_portgroup.description: Create a VMware Port Group on a VMware Standard Switch (vSS) for given ESXi host(s) or hosts of given cluster.

vlan_id.description: VLAN ID to assign to portgroup. Set to 0 (no VLAN tagging) by default.

security.description: Network policy specifies layer 2 security settings for a portgroup such as promiscuous mode, where guest adapter listens to all the packets, MAC address changes and forged transmits. Dict which configures the different security values for portgroup.

teaming.description: Dictionary which configures the different teaming values for portgroup.

traffic_shaping.description: Dictionary which configures traffic shaping for the switch.

cluster_name.description: Name of cluster name for host membership. Portgroup will be created on all hosts of the given cluster. This option is required if O(hosts) is not specified.

hosts.description: List of name of host or hosts on which portgroup needs to be added. This option is required if O(cluster_name) is not specified.

state.description: Determines if the portgroup should be present or not.

community.vmware.vmware_portgroup_info.short_description: Gathers info about an ESXi host's Port Group configuration
community.vmware.vmware_portgroup_info.description: This module can be used to gather information about an ESXi host's Port Group configuration when ESXi hostname or Cluster name is given.

policies.description: Gather information about Security, Traffic Shaping, as well as Teaming and failover. The property C(ts) stands for Traffic Shaping and C(lb) for Load Balancing.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster. Info will be returned for all hostsystem belonging to this cluster name. If O(esxi_hostname) is not given, this parameter is required.

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname to gather information from. If O(cluster_name) is not given, this parameter is required.

community.vmware.vmware_recommended_data.short_description: Returns the recommended datastore from a SDRS-enabled datastore cluster
community.vmware.vmware_recommended_data.description: This module provides the recommended datastore name from a datastore cluster only if the SDRS is enabled for the specified datastore cluster

datacenter.description: Name of the datacenter.

community.vmware.vmware_resource_pool.description: This module can be used to add/remove a resource pool to/from vCenter

datacenter.description: Name of the datacenter.

cluster.description: Name of the cluster to configure the resource pool. This parameter is required if O(esxi_hostname) or O(parent_resource_pool) is not specified. The O(cluster), O(esxi_hostname) and O(parent_resource_pool) parameters are mutually exclusive.

esxi_hostname.description: Name of the host to configure the resource pool. The host must not be member of a cluster. This parameter is required if O(cluster) or O(parent_resource_pool) is not specified. The O(cluster), O(esxi_hostname) and O(parent_resource_pool) parameters are mutually exclusive.

parent_resource_pool.description: Name of the parent resource pool. This parameter is required if O(cluster) or O(esxi_hostname) is not specified. The O(cluster), O(esxi_hostname) and O(parent_resource_pool) parameters are mutually exclusive.

cpu_expandable_reservations.description: In a resource pool with an expandable reservation, the reservation on a resource pool can grow beyond the specified value.

cpu_reservation.description: Amount of resource that is guaranteed available to the virtual machine or resource pool.

cpu_limit.description: The utilization of a virtual machine/resource pool will not exceed this limit, even if there are available resources. The default value -1 indicates no limit.

cpu_shares.description: Memory shares are used in case of resource contention.

cpu_allocation_shares.description: The number of cpu shares allocated. This value is only set if O(cpu_shares=custom).

mem_expandable_reservations.description: In a resource pool with an expandable reservation, the reservation on a resource pool can grow beyond the specified value.

mem_reservation.description: Amount of resource that is guaranteed available to the virtual machine or resource pool.

mem_limit.description: The utilization of a virtual machine/resource pool will not exceed this limit, even if there are available resources. The default value -1 indicates no limit.

mem_shares.description: Memory shares are used in case of resource contention.

mem_allocation_shares.description: The number of memory shares allocated. This value is only set if O(mem_shares=custom).

state.description: Add or remove the resource pool

community.vmware.vmware_resource_pool_in.short_description: Gathers info about resource pool information
community.vmware.vmware_resource_pool_in.description: This module can be used to gather information about all resource configuration information.

community.vmware.vmware_sdrs_vm_override.short_description: Check for VM overrides in a SDRS datastore cluster
community.vmware.vmware_sdrs_vm_override.description: This module can be used to check if VM overrides exist in a SDRS enabled datastore cluster in a given VMware environment. All parameters and VMware object values are case sensitive.

datastore_cluster_name.description: The name of the datastore cluster.

community.vmware.vmware_tag.description: This module can be used to create / delete / update VMware tags. Tag feature is introduced in vSphere 6 version, so this module is not supported in the earlier versions of vSphere.

tag_name.description: The name of tag to manage.

tag_description.description: The tag description. This is required only if O(state=present). This parameter is ignored, when O(state=absent). Process of updating tag only allows description change.

category_id.description: The unique ID generated by vCenter should be used to. User can get this unique ID from facts module. Required if O(category_name) is not set.

category_name.description: The name of category. Required if O(category_id) is not set.

state.description: The state of tag. If set to V(present) and tag does not exists, then tag is created. If set to V(present) and tag exists, then tag is updated. If set to V(absent) and tag exists, then tag is deleted. If set to V(absent) and tag does not exists, no action is taken.

community.vmware.vmware_tag_info.description: This module can be used to collect information about VMware tags. Tag feature is introduced in vSphere 6 version, so this module is not supported in the earlier versions of vSphere.

community.vmware.vmware_tag_manager.short_description: Manage association of VMware tags with VMware objects
community.vmware.vmware_tag_manager.description: This module can be used to assign / remove VMware tags from the given VMware objects. Tag feature is introduced in vSphere 6 version, so this module is not supported in the earlier versions of vSphere.

tag_names.description: List of tag(s) to be managed. User can also specify category name by specifying colon separated value. For example, "category_name:tag_name". User can also specify tag and category as dict, when tag or category contains colon. See example for more information. User can skip category name if you have unique tag names.

state.description: If set to V(add) or V(present) will add the tags to the existing tag list of the given object. If set to V(remove) or V(absent) will remove the tags from the existing tag list of the given object. If set to V(set) will replace the tags of the given objects with the user defined list of tags.

object_type.description: Type of object to work with.

object_name.description: Name of the object to work with. For DistributedVirtualPortgroups the format should be "switch_name:portgroup_name" Required if O(moid) is not set.

moid.description: Managed object ID for the given object. Required if O(object_name) is not set.

community.vmware.vmware_target_canonical.short_description: Return canonical (NAA) from an ESXi host system
community.vmware.vmware_target_canonical.description: This module can be used to gather information about canonical (NAA) from an ESXi host based on SCSI target ID.

target_id.description: The target id based on order of scsi device. version 2.6 onwards, this parameter is optional.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster. Info about all SCSI devices for all host system in the given cluster is returned. This parameter is required, if O(esxi_hostname) is not provided.

esxi_hostname.description: Name of the ESXi host system. Info about all SCSI devices for the given ESXi host system is returned. This parameter is required, if O(cluster_name) is not provided.

community.vmware.vmware_vasa.short_description: Manage VMware Virtual Volumes storage provider
community.vmware.vmware_vasa.description: This module can be used to register and unregister a VASA provider

vasa_name.description: The name of the VASA provider to be managed.

vasa_url.description: The url  of the VASA provider to be managed. This parameter is required if O(state=present)

vasa_username.description: The user account to connect to the VASA provider. This parameter is required if O(state=present)

vasa_password.description: The password of the user account to connect to the VASA provider. This parameter is required if O(state=present)

vasa_certificate.description: The SSL certificate of the VASA provider. This parameter is required if O(state=present)

state.description: Create (V(present)) or remove (V(absent)) a VASA provider.

community.vmware.vmware_vasa_info.short_description: Gather information about vSphere VASA providers.
community.vmware.vmware_vasa_info.description: Returns basic information on the vSphere VASA providers registered in the vcenter.

community.vmware.vmware_vc_infraprofile_.short_description: List and Export VMware vCenter infra profile configs.
community.vmware.vmware_vc_infraprofile_.description: Module to manage VMware vCenter infra profile configs. vCenter infra profile Library feature is introduced in vSphere 7.0 version, so this module is not supported in the earlier versions of vSphere.

decryption_key.description: decryption_key argument for while doing import profile task as of now its not taken into account form API team.

encryption_key.description: encryption_key argument for while doing import profile task as of now its not taken into account form API team.

api.description: API which needs to be executed

config_path.description: Config file path which contains infra profile config JSON data, supports both relative and absolute path. This parameter is required only when V(import),V(validate) APIs are being used.

profiles.description: A list of profile names to be exported, imported, and validated. This parameter is not required while running for List API, not for V(export),V(import) and V(validate).

description.description: Description of about encryption or decryption key.

community.vmware.vmware_vcenter_settings.short_description: Configures general settings on a vCenter server
community.vmware.vmware_vcenter_settings.description: This module can be used to configure the vCenter server general settings (except the statistics). The statistics can be configured with the module M(community.vmware.vmware_vcenter_statistics).

database.description: The database settings for vCenter server.

runtime_settings.description: The unique runtime settings for vCenter server.

user_directory.description: The user directory settings for the vCenter server installation.

mail.description: The settings vCenter server uses to send email alerts.

snmp_receivers.description: SNMP trap destinations for vCenter server alerts.

timeout_settings.description: The vCenter server connection timeout for normal and long operations.

logging_options.description: The level of detail that vCenter server usesfor log files.

community.vmware.vmware_vcenter_settings.description: This module can be used to gather information about vCenter settings.

schema.description: Specify the output schema desired. The 'summary' output schema is the legacy output from the module. The 'vsphere' output schema is the vSphere API class definition.

properties.description: Specify the properties to retrieve. Example:    properties: [       "config.workflow.port"    ] Only valid when O(schema=vsphere).

community.vmware.vmware_vcenter_statisti.short_description: Configures statistics on a vCenter server
community.vmware.vmware_vcenter_statisti.description: This module can be used to configure the vCenter server statistics. The remaining settings can be configured with the module M(community.vmware.vmware_vcenter_settings).

interval_past_day.description: Settings for vCenter server past day statistic collection.

interval_past_week.description: Settings for vCenter server past week statistic collection.

interval_past_month.description: Settings for vCenter server past month statistic collection.

interval_past_year.description: Settings for vCenter server past month statistic collection.

community.vmware.vmware_vm_config_option.short_description: Return supported guest ID list and VM recommended config option for specific guest OS
community.vmware.vmware_vm_config_option.description: This module is used for getting the hardware versions supported for creation, the guest ID list supported by ESXi host for the most recent virtual hardware supported or specified hardware version, the VM recommended config options for specified guest OS ID.

datacenter.description: The datacenter name used to get specified cluster or host.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster. If O(esxi_hostname) is not given, this parameter is required.

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname. Obtain VM configure options on this ESXi host. If O(cluster_name) is not given, this parameter is required.

get_hardware_versions.description: Return the list of VM hardware versions supported for creation and the default hardware version on the specified entity.

get_guest_os_ids.description: Return the list of guest OS IDs supported on the specified entity. If O(hardware_version) is set, will return the corresponding guest OS ID list supported, or will return the guest OS ID list for the default hardware version.

get_config_options.description: Return the dict of VM recommended config options for guest ID specified by O(guest_id) with hardware version specified by O(hardware_version) or the default hardware version. When set to V(true), O(guest_id) must be set.

guest_id.description: The guest OS ID from the returned list when O(get_guest_os_ids=true), e.g., 'rhel8_64Guest'. This parameter must be set when O(get_config_options=true).

hardware_version.description: The hardware version from the returned list when O(get_hardware_versions=true), e.g., 'vmx-19'.

community.vmware.vmware_vm_host_drs_rule.short_description: Creates vm/host group in a given cluster
community.vmware.vmware_vm_host_drs_rule.description: This module can be used to create VM-Host rules in a given cluster.
