affinity_rule.description: If set to V(true), the DRS rule will be an Affinity rule. If set to V(false), the DRS rule will be an Anti-Affinity rule. Effective only if O(state=present).

datacenter.description: Datacenter to search for given cluster. If not set, we use first cluster we encounter with O(cluster_name).

cluster_name.description: Cluster to create VM-Host rule.

drs_rule_name.description: Name of rule to create or remove.

enabled.description: If set to V(true), the DRS rule will be enabled. Effective only if O(state=present).

host_group_name.description: Name of Host group to use with rule. Effective only if O(state=present).

mandatory.description: If set to V(true), the DRS rule will be mandatory. Effective only if O(state=present).

state.description: If set to V(present) and the rule does not exist then the rule will be created. If set to V(absent) and the rule exists then the rule will be deleted.

vm_group_name.description: Name of VM group to use with rule. Effective only if O(state=present).

community.vmware.vmware_vm_info.short_description: Return basic info pertaining to a VMware machine guest
community.vmware.vmware_vm_info.description: Return basic information pertaining to a vSphere or ESXi virtual machine guest.

vm_type.description: If set to V(vm), then information are gathered for virtual machines only. If set to V(template), then information are gathered for virtual machine templates only. If set to V(all), then information are gathered for all virtual machines and virtual machine templates.

show_attribute.description: Attributes related to VM guest shown in information only when this is set V(true).

folder.description: Specify a folder location of VMs to gather information from. Examples:    folder: /ha-datacenter/vm    folder: ha-datacenter/vm    folder: /datacenter1/vm    folder: datacenter1/vm    folder: /datacenter1/vm/folder1    folder: datacenter1/vm/folder1    folder: /folder1/datacenter1/vm    folder: folder1/datacenter1/vm    folder: /folder1/datacenter1/vm/folder2

show_cluster.description: Tags virtual machine's cluster is shown if set to V(true).

show_datacenter.description: Tags virtual machine's datacenter is shown if set to V(true).

show_datastore.description: Tags virtual machine's datastore is shown if set to V(true).

show_esxi_hostname.description: Tags virtual machine's ESXi host is shown if set to V(true).

show_mac_address.description: Tags virtual machine's mac address is shown if set to V(true).

show_net.description: Tags virtual machine's network is shown if set to V(true).

show_resource_pool.description: Tags virtual machine's resource pool is shown if set to V(true).

show_tag.description: Tags related to virtual machine are shown if set to V(true).

show_allocated.description: Allocated storage in byte and memory in MB are shown if it set to True.

vm_name.description: Name of the virtual machine to get related configurations information from.

community.vmware.vmware_vm_shell.short_description: Run commands in a VMware guest operating system
community.vmware.vmware_vm_shell.description: Module allows user to run common system administration commands in the guest operating system.

datacenter.description: The datacenter hosting the virtual machine. If set, it will help to speed up virtual machine search.

cluster.description: The cluster hosting the virtual machine. If set, it will help to speed up virtual machine search.

folder.description: Destination folder, absolute or relative path to find an existing guest or create the new guest. The folder should include the datacenter. ESX's datacenter is ha-datacenter. Examples:    folder: /ha-datacenter/vm    folder: ha-datacenter/vm    folder: /datacenter1/vm    folder: datacenter1/vm    folder: /datacenter1/vm/folder1    folder: datacenter1/vm/folder1    folder: /folder1/datacenter1/vm    folder: folder1/datacenter1/vm    folder: /folder1/datacenter1/vm/folder2 Required if O(vm_id_type=inventory_path)

vm_id.description: Name of the virtual machine to work with.

vm_id_type.description: The VMware identification method by which the virtual machine will be identified.

vm_username.description: The user to login-in to the virtual machine.

vm_password.description: The password used to login-in to the virtual machine.

vm_shell.description: The absolute path to the program to start. On Linux, shell is executed via bash.

vm_shell_args.description: The argument to the program. The characters which must be escaped to the shell also be escaped on the command line provided.

vm_shell_env.description: Comma separated list of environment variable, specified in the guest OS notation.

vm_shell_cwd.description: The current working directory of the application from which it will be run.

wait_for_process.description: If set to V(true), module will wait for process to complete in the given virtual machine.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds. If set to positive integers, then O(wait_for_process=true) will honor this parameter and will exit after this timeout.

community.vmware.vmware_vm_storage_polic.description: A vSphere storage policy defines metadata that describes storage requirements for virtual machines and storage capabilities of storage providers. Currently, only tag-based storage policy creation is supported.

name.description: Name of the storage policy to create, update, or delete.

description.description: Description of the storage policy to create or update. This parameter is ignored when O(state=absent).

tag_category.description: Name of the pre-existing tag category to assign to the storage policy. This parameter is ignored when O(state=absent). This parameter is required when O(state=present).

tag_name.description: Name of the pre-existing tag to assign to the storage policy. This parameter is ignored when O(state=absent). This parameter is required when O(state=present).

tag_affinity.description: If set to V(true), the storage policy enforces that virtual machines require the existence of a tag for datastore placement. If set to V(false), the storage policy enforces that virtual machines require the absence of a tag for datastore placement. This parameter is ignored when O(state=absent).

state.description: State of storage policy. If set to V(present), the storage policy is created. If set to V(absent), the storage policy is deleted.

community.vmware.vmware_vm_storage_polic.short_description: Gather information about vSphere storage profile defined storage policy information.
community.vmware.vmware_vm_storage_polic.description: Returns basic information on vSphere storage profiles. A vSphere storage profile defines storage policy information that describes storage requirements for virtual machines and storage capabilities of storage providers.

community.vmware.vmware_vm_vm_drs_rule.short_description: Configure VMware DRS Affinity rule for virtual machines in the given cluster
community.vmware.vmware_vm_vm_drs_rule.description: This module can be used to configure VMware DRS Affinity rule for virtual machines in the given cluster.

datacenter.description: Datacenter to search for given cluster. If not set, we use first cluster we encounter with O(cluster_name).

cluster_name.description: Desired cluster name where virtual machines are present for the DRS rule.

vms.description: List of virtual machines name for which DRS rule needs to be applied. Required if O(state=present).

drs_rule_name.description: The name of the DRS rule to manage.

enabled.description: If set to V(true), the DRS rule will be enabled. Effective only if O(state=present).

mandatory.description: If set to V(true), the DRS rule will be mandatory. Effective only if O(state=present).

affinity_rule.description: If set to V(true), the DRS rule will be an Affinity rule. If set to V(false), the DRS rule will be an Anti-Affinity rule. Effective only if O(state=present).

state.description: If set to V(present), then the DRS rule is created if not present. If set to V(present), then the DRS rule is already present, it updates to the given configurations. If set to V(absent), then the DRS rule is deleted if present.

community.vmware.vmware_vm_vss_dvs_migra.short_description: Migrates a virtual machine from a standard vswitch to distributed
community.vmware.vmware_vm_vss_dvs_migra.description: Migrates a virtual machine from a standard vswitch to distributed

vm_name.description: Name of the virtual machine to migrate to a dvSwitch

dvportgroup_name.description: Name of the portgroup to migrate to the virtual machine to

community.vmware.vmware_vmkernel.short_description: Manages a VMware VMkernel Adapter of an ESXi host.
community.vmware.vmware_vmkernel.description: This module can be used to manage the VMKernel adapters / VMKernel network interfaces of an ESXi host. The module assumes that the host is already configured with the Port Group in case of a vSphere Standard Switch (vSS). The module assumes that the host is already configured with the Distributed Port Group in case of a vSphere Distributed Switch (vDS). The module automatically migrates the VMKernel adapter from vSS to vDS or vice versa if present.

vswitch_name.description: The name of the vSwitch where to add the VMKernel interface. Required parameter only if O(state=present). Optional parameter from version 2.5 and onwards.

dvswitch_name.description: The name of the vSphere Distributed Switch (vDS) where to add the VMKernel interface. Required parameter only if O(state=present). Optional parameter from version 2.8 and onwards.

portgroup_name.description: The name of the port group for the VMKernel interface.

mtu.description: The MTU for the VMKernel interface. The default value of 1500 is valid from version 2.5 and onwards.

device.description: Search VMkernel adapter by device name. The parameter is required only in case of O(network.type=dhcp).

enable_vsan.description: Enable VSAN traffic on the VMKernel adapter. This option is only allowed if the default TCP/IP stack is used.

enable_vmotion.description: Enable vMotion traffic on the VMKernel adapter. This option is only allowed if the default TCP/IP stack is used. You cannot enable vMotion on an additional adapter if you already have an adapter with the vMotion TCP/IP stack configured.

enable_mgmt.description: Enable Management traffic on the VMKernel adapter. This option is only allowed if the default TCP/IP stack is used.

enable_ft.description: Enable Fault Tolerance traffic on the VMKernel adapter. This option is only allowed if the default TCP/IP stack is used.

enable_provisioning.description: Enable Provisioning traffic on the VMKernel adapter. This option is only allowed if the default TCP/IP stack is used.

enable_replication.description: Enable vSphere Replication traffic on the VMKernel adapter. This option is only allowed if the default TCP/IP stack is used.

enable_replication_nfc.description: Enable vSphere Replication NFC traffic on the VMKernel adapter. This option is only allowed if the default TCP/IP stack is used.

enable_backup_nfc.description: Enable vSphere Backup NFC traffic on the VMKernel adapter. This option is only allowed if the default TCP/IP stack is used.

state.description: If set to V(present), the VMKernel adapter will be created with the given specifications. If set to V(absent), the VMKernel adapter will be removed. If set to V(present) and VMKernel adapter exists, the configurations will be updated.

esxi_hostname.description: Name of ESXi host to which VMKernel is to be managed.

community.vmware.vmware_vmkernel_info.short_description: Gathers VMKernel info about an ESXi host
community.vmware.vmware_vmkernel_info.description: This module can be used to gather VMKernel information about an ESXi host from given ESXi hostname or cluster name.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster. VMKernel information about each ESXi server will be returned for the given cluster. If O(esxi_hostname) is not given, this parameter is required.

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname. VMKernel information about this ESXi server will be returned. If O(cluster_name) is not given, this parameter is required.

community.vmware.vmware_vmotion.short_description: Move a virtual machine using vMotion, and/or its vmdks using storage vMotion.
community.vmware.vmware_vmotion.description: Using VMware vCenter, move a virtual machine using vMotion to a different host, and/or its vmdks to another datastore using storage vMotion.

vm_name.description: Name of the VM to perform a vMotion on. This is required parameter, if O(vm_uuid) is not set.

vm_uuid.description: UUID of the virtual machine to perform a vMotion operation on. This is a required parameter, if O(vm_name) or O(moid) is not set.

moid.description: Managed Object ID of the instance to manage if known, this is a unique identifier only within a single vCenter instance. This is required if O(vm_name) or O(vm_uuid) is not supplied.

use_instance_uuid.description: Whether to use the VMware instance UUID rather than the BIOS UUID.

destination_host.description: Name of the destination host the virtual machine should be running on. Version 2.6 onwards, this parameter is not a required parameter, unlike the previous versions.

destination_cluster.description: Name of the destination cluster the virtual machine should be running on. Only works if drs is enabled for this cluster.

destination_datastore_cluster.description: Name of the destination datastore cluster (storage pod) the virtual machine's vmdk should be moved on. Only works if drs is enabled for the cluster the vm is running / should run.

destination_datastore.description: Name of the destination datastore the virtual machine's vmdk should be moved on.

destination_datacenter.description: Name of the destination datacenter the datastore is located on. Optional, required only when datastores are shared across datacenters.

destination_resourcepool.description: Name of the destination resource pool where the virtual machine should be running. Resource pool is required if vmotion is done between hosts which are part of different clusters or datacenters. if not passed, resource_pool object will be retrived from host_obj parent.

timeout.description: The timeout in seconds. When the timeout is reached, the module will fail.

community.vmware.vmware_vsan_cluster.short_description: Configure VSAN clustering on an ESXi host
community.vmware.vmware_vsan_cluster.description: This module can be used to configure VSAN clustering on an ESXi host

community.vmware.vmware_vsan_hcl_db.short_description: Manages the vSAN Hardware Compatibility List (HCL) database
community.vmware.vmware_vsan_hcl_db.description: Manages vSAN HCL db on vSphere DB file can be downloaded from https://partnerweb.vmware.com/service/vsan/all.json

source.description: The path to the HCL db file

community.vmware.vmware_vsan_health_info.short_description: Gather information about a VMware vSAN cluster's health
community.vmware.vmware_vsan_health_info.description: Gather information about a VMware vSAN cluster's health.

datacenter.description: Name of the Datacenter.

cluster_name.description: Name of the vSAN cluster.

fetch_from_cache.description: V(true) to return the result from cache directly instead of running the full health check.

community.vmware.vmware_vsan_release_cat.description: Manually upload the vSAN Release Catalog the the vCenter See https://kb.vmware.com/s/article/58891 for more details

source.description: The path to the release catalog file

community.vmware.vmware_vspan_session.short_description: Create or remove a Port Mirroring session.
community.vmware.vmware_vspan_session.description: This module can be used to create, delete or edit different kind of port mirroring sessions.

switch.description: The name of the distributed vSwitch on which to add or remove the mirroring session.

name.description: Name of the session.

state.description: Create or remove the session.

session_type.description: Select the mirroring type. In V(encapsulatedRemoteMirrorSource) session, Distributed Ports can be used as source entities, and IP address can be used as destination entities. In V(remoteMirrorDest) session, VLAN IDs can be used as source entities, and Distributed Ports can be used as destination entities. In V(remoteMirrorSource) session, Distributed Ports can be used as source entities, and uplink ports name can be used as destination entities. In V(dvPortMirror) session, Distributed Ports can be used as both source and destination entities.

source_port_transmitted.description: Source port for which transmitted packets are mirrored.

source_port_received.description: Source port for which received packets are mirrored.

destination_port.description: Destination port that received the mirrored packets. Also any port designated in the value of this property can not match the source port in any of the Distributed Port Mirroring session.

encapsulation_vlan_id.description: VLAN ID used to encapsulate the mirrored traffic.

strip_original_vlan.description: Whether to strip the original VLAN tag. If false, the original VLAN tag will be preserved on the mirrored traffic. If O(encapsulation_vlan_id) has been set and this property is V(false), the frames will be double tagged with the original VLAN ID as the inner tag.

mirrored_packet_length.description: An integer that describes how much of each frame to mirror. If unset, all of the frame would be mirrored. Setting this property to a smaller value is useful when the consumer will look only at the headers. The value cannot be less than 60.

normal_traffic_allowed.description: Whether or not destination ports can send and receive "normal" traffic. Setting this to false will make mirror ports be used solely for mirroring and not double as normal access ports.

sampling_rate.description: Sampling rate of the session. If its value is n, one of every n packets is mirrored. Valid values are between 1 to 65535.

source_vm_transmitted.description: With this parameter it is possible, to add a NIC of a VM to a port mirroring session.

source_vm_received.description: With this parameter it is possible, to add a NIC of a VM to a port mirroring session.

destination_vm.description: With this parameter it is possible, to add a NIC of a VM to a port mirroring session.

community.vmware.vmware_vswitch.short_description: Manage a VMware Standard Switch to an ESXi host.
community.vmware.vmware_vswitch.description: This module can be used to add, remove and update a VMware Standard Switch to an ESXi host.

nics.description: A list of vmnic names or vmnic name to attach to vSwitch.

number_of_ports.description: Number of port to configure on vSwitch.

state.description: Add or remove the switch.

esxi_hostname.description: Manage the vSwitch using this ESXi host system.

security.description: Network policy specifies layer 2 security settings for a portgroup such as promiscuous mode, where guest adapter listens to all the packets, MAC address changes and forged transmits. Dict which configures the different security values for portgroup.

teaming.description: Dictionary which configures the different teaming values for portgroup.

traffic_shaping.description: Dictionary which configures traffic shaping for the switch.

community.vmware.vmware_vswitch_info.short_description: Gathers info about an ESXi host's vswitch configurations
community.vmware.vmware_vswitch_info.description: This module can be used to gather information about an ESXi host's vswitch configurations when ESXi hostname or Cluster name is given. The vSphere Client shows the value for the number of ports as elastic from vSphere 5.5 and above. Other tools like esxcli might show the number of ports as 1536 or 5632. See U(https://kb.vmware.com/s/article/2064511) for more details.

policies.description: Gather information about Security, Traffic Shaping, as well as Teaming and failover. The property C(ts) stands for Traffic Shaping and C(lb) for Load Balancing.

cluster_name.description: Name of the cluster. Info about vswitch belonging to every ESXi host systems under this cluster will be returned. If O(esxi_hostname) is not given, this parameter is required.

esxi_hostname.description: ESXi hostname to gather information from. If O(cluster_name) is not given, this parameter is required.

community.vmware.vsan_health_silent_chec.description: Take a list of vSAN health checks and silence them Re-enable alerts for previously silenced health checks

cluster_name.description: Name of the vSAN cluster.

state.description: The state of the health checks. If set to V(present), all given health checks will be silenced. If set to V(absent), all given health checks will be removed from the list of silent checks.

community.vmware.vsphere_copy.short_description: Copy a file to a VMware datastore
community.vmware.vsphere_copy.description: Upload files to a VMware datastore through a vCenter REST API.

src.description: The file to push to vCenter.

datacenter.description: The datacenter on the vCenter server that holds the datastore.

datastore.description: The datastore to push files to.

path.description: The file to push to the datastore.

timeout.description: The timeout in seconds for the upload to the datastore.

diskformat.description: Optional argument - Set a diskformat for certain uploads like stream optimized VMDKs There is no official documentation, but it looks like V(StreamVmdk) needs to be set for stream optimized VMDKs that are uploaded to vSAN storage Setting this for non-VMDK files might lead to undefined behavior and is not supported.

community.vmware.vsphere_file.short_description: Manage files on a vCenter datastore
community.vmware.vsphere_file.description: Manage files on a vCenter datastore.

datacenter.description: The datacenter on the vCenter server that holds the datastore.

datastore.description: The datastore on the vCenter server to push files to.

path.description: The file or directory on the datastore on the vCenter server.

timeout.description: The timeout in seconds for the upload to the datastore.

state.description: The state of or the action on the provided path. If V(absent), the file will be removed. If V(directory), the directory will be created. If V(file), more information of the (existing) file will be returned. If V(touch), an empty file will be created if the path does not exist.
